content.subject: Re: Unable to configure SMTP relay for my domain

body.text:
Hi Jane,

Great to hear that you've added your IP range to the whitelist!

IP whitelist propagation typically takes just around 15 minutes to complete. Please wait for about 15 minutes and then try your SMTP relay connection again.

If you continue to experience authentication errors after that time, please let us know and we can investigate further.

Best regards,
Support Team
body.html:
Hi Jane,

Great to hear that you've added your IP range to the whitelist!

IP whitelist propagation typically takes just around 15 minutes to complete. Please wait for about 15 minutes and then try your SMTP relay connection again.

If you continue to experience authentication errors after that time, please let us know and we can investigate further.

Best regards,
Support Team

content.subject: Re: Unable to configure SMTP relay for my domain

body.text:
Hi Jane,

Thanks for the update! IP whitelist propagation typically takes about 15 minutes to fully sync across our systems. Please wait a few minutes and then try configuring your SMTP relay again.

Let us know if you still encounter any issues after the retry. We're here to help!

Best regards,
Support Team
body.html:
Hi Jane,

Thanks for the update! IP whitelist propagation typically takes about 15 minutes to fully sync across our systems. Please wait a few minutes and then try configuring your SMTP relay again.

Let us know if you still encounter any issues after the retry. We're here to help!

Best regards,
Support Team

content.subject: [Incident INC-2024-0821-A1] Service Degradation - us-east-1 Region
content.body_text:
Dear Customer,

We are currently experiencing a service degradation affecting connectivity in the us-east-1 region (North Virginia / DC Metro). Our team is actively working to mitigate the issue.

Impact Details:
- Region: us-east-1 (POP-EQX-ASH1, POP-EQX-NYC1)
- Issue: Partial outage affecting Layer 2 Virtual Circuits and Cloud Exchange Direct Connect
- Root Cause: Upstream fiber utility cut

Actions Taken:
- We are proactively rerouting affected traffic through secondary gateways
- Engaging with upstream fiber provider for repair ETA

We will provide further updates as they become available. Thank you for your patience.

Regards,
TaaS Operations Team

business_impact.notes: Primary circuit down for high-priority customer CUST-882310. Backup path active. Two additional customers experiencing degraded performance.

metadata_updates.original_error_note: Initial report mentioned APAC but analysis confirmed impact is in us-east-1

predicted_params.status_reason: Corrected impact scope from APAC to us-east-1 based on scope analysis

expected_params.summary: Corrected affected region from APAC to us-east-1 based on scope analysis
expected_params.details: Initial incident creation indicated APAC region as affected. Scope analysis confirmed actual impact is in us-east-1 (North Virginia / DC Metro), affecting sites POP-EQX-ASH1 and POP-EQX-NYC1.

Access control rules for Payment and User Profile APIs.

expected_params.name: Fix NY-BOS Congestion - Phase 1
expected_params.description: Reroute 20% of non-critical traffic from the congested primary link (CR-London-Primary-04 -> ES-Manchester-Agg-01) to the secondary link via CR-London-Secondary-02. This will alleviate congestion during peak hours (18:00-22:00), reduce latency from 45ms to baseline levels (~12ms), and mitigate SLA penalty risk for FinTech Global Ltd ($15,000 at risk).

business_case.justification: High SLA penalty risk of $45,000 total, with $15,000 immediately at risk for FinTech Global Ltd. Active incident inc_8821_net_slow is correlated with this congestion. Rerouting 20% non-critical traffic will reduce utilization from 94.2% to ~75%, bringing latency back within SLA thresholds.

predicted_params.description: Reroute 20% of non-critical traffic via secondary link CR-London-Secondary-02 to alleviate congestion on CR-London-Primary-04 and reduce SLA penalty risk for enterprise customers.

business_case.justification: Avoid potential SLA penalty of $45,000 and reduce network congestion affecting 15,420 subscribers. Directly addresses active incident inc_8821_net_slow.

components.description: 20% discount on monthly bill for 12 months when upgrading to Fiber 500 plan
Speed upgrade from Fiber 300 to Fiber 500 (1Gbps capable)
One-time goodwill credit of $50 for service interruptions experienced in the last 90 days

expected_params.notes_for_agent: This bundled offer addresses the customer's top 3 concerns: (1) Network performance - upgrade to Fiber 500 resolves speed issues, (2) Price sensitivity - 20% discount for 12 months beats competitor's $20 gap, (3) Service frustration - $50 goodwill credit acknowledges past service interruptions. Emphasize the price lock guarantee and technical improvement during the call. Note: A senior technician dispatch for line quality check should be scheduled BEFORE or IMMEDIATELY AFTER offer acceptance to ensure speed improvement is delivered.
